rulings.Case Title: WING WO MA VS. JUN TAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 3, 2011, Line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTS JUN TAN AND LINDA HO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is off calendar as moot. Cross-complainants' allegation that the actual construction work was done by plaintiff, who is not a licensed contractor, strongly suggests that cross-complainants are not owed any money for the work and are seeking to collect for work performed by a person who is legally unable to collect for construction work. The further allegation that plaintiff's "status in the eyes of the law was that as an employee of" the individual cross-complainant is both a conclusion not based on any ultimate facts and incomprehensible without some explanation as to why plaintiff is or should be deemed an employee of the individual cross-complainant. Cross-complainants are granted leave, if they can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts why they are owed money for the construction work performed by plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI et al VS. PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 3, 2011, Line 4. DEFENDANTS ADAM SPARKS AND PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Denied. The new claims alleged in the second amended complaint are not within the scope of the 2002 agreement and, even if they were, defendants waived their right to arbitrate those claims by their own litigation activities in this case and their delay in bringing this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KIN FONG VS. LAWRENCE WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Mtn To Compel Deft To Further Respond To Pltf'S Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 3, 2011, Line 5. PLAINTIFF KIN FONG'S (RE-NOTICE) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTEROGATORIES. The parties are directed to engage in good faith meet and confer efforts and report on their progress in doing so at the hearing. At a minimum plaintiff should provide definitions for specially defined terms and defendant should reframe all discovery responses that they genuinely believe are too ambiguous to respond to as written into language that they can respond to in light of what they genuinely understand to be at issue in the case. rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN STACK VS. KENDRA STACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 3, 2011, Line 14. PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN STACK'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF PARTITION (PART ONE) Granted. The property shall be sold. The proceeds of the sale shall be divided in accordance with each party's 50% interest in the property. The costs of the sale, including the costs of the referee, shall also be evenly divided between the parties. The following three persons are nominated as possible referees: 1) Michael Kasolas (992-5806), who is the husband of a court employee that does not work in department 302; 2) Everett Hewlett (772-0900), who is a former commissioner of the court; and 3) Emilie Calhoun (759-8755), an attorney who handles cases that are in the court's probate department. The parties may contact the nominees to learn about the nominees' rates, qualifications and whether any of them have had any contacts with any of the parties, their counsel or others who have been involved in this case, but may not make inquiries beyond these areas. The parties have until November 14 to choose of these three or anyone else they can agree on to be the referee. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN STACK VS. KENDRA STACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Kathleen Stack'S Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition (Part Two)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 3, 2011, Line 14. PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN STACK'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF PARTITION (PART TWO). If the parties do not agree on a referee by November 14, then each party may, by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org without copying the other side, on or before November 16 strike one of the nominees and the court will thereafter appoint a referee from the nominee(s) not stricken. The referee is empowered to hire a real estate broker and, in consultation with that broker, to decide the method (sale of the entire property or split up) that is likely to achieve the maximum net proceeds for the parties. Neither Charlene Delaney nor Keller Williams Realty shall be selected as the real estate broker. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TINA CHEN et al VS. RECOLOGY INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, November 3, Line 15, DEFENDANTs RECOLOGY INC., RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend the second cause of action to allege, if the plaintiffs can do so in good faith, that they owned, leased, occupied or controlled the property where the tree was located at the time of the incident. (Hassoldt v. Patrick Media Group, Inc. (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 153, 171; CACI 2002(1).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
